label: true-dup
a: Canary deployment stages: new version starts at 5% of live traffic, monitored for 30 minutes. If p99 latency and error rate stay within 10% of baseline, traffic moves to 20%, then another 30-minute window, then 50%, then 100%. Any metric spike during any window triggers automatic rollback to the previous version.
b: New releases roll out in four stages: 5% → 20% → 50% → 100%. Each stage has a 30-minute observation window. The system watches p99 response time and error rate. If either climbs more than 10% above the stable baseline for the previous version, Kong automatically shifts all traffic back and alerts the team.
reviewer_notes: Both describe the same canary rollout: 5/20/50/100% stages, 30-minute windows, 10% threshold, auto-rollback at Kong.
